title: Aroma
requirements: A Wii U on the latest firmware (5.5.6 or lower in North America, 5.5.5 or lower elsewhere)
An SD card, 32GB or larger recommended (a microSD with an adapter is fine)
The Wii U connected to the internet
warnings: Every time you modify a Wii U there is a !!small chance of an unrecoverable brick!!. Follow each step exactly.
!!Do NOT!! rename your SD card to 'wiiu' because it !!breaks homebrew!!.
Make a NAND backup when the guide tells you to. Don't skip it.
_note: Downloads come live from aroma.foryour.cafe (the official Aroma site). Files and versions are always the latest the site serves.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
This installs **Aroma**, the current custom firmware for the Wii U. It loads every time you turn the console on and lets you run homebrew straight from the Wii U menu.

Graphite handles the file downloads and copies them to your SD card for you. The rest is a handful of button presses on the Wii U itself, and I'll walk you through each one with a picture.

Two things to check first: your Wii U is on the **latest firmware**, and your SD card is **FAT32** (Graphite verifies this below). Never name the SD card `wiiu`.

steps.title: Put the Aroma files on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite will now download the two official Aroma packages (the **payloads** and the **base Aroma** files) and unpack both onto your SD card in the right folders.

This is everything the guide would normally have you download by hand and drag over. Just press the button and wait.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS quietly scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii U homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii U
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of your computer and slot it into the **Wii U**.

Make sure the console is connected to the internet because the next step uses the browser. Press **I did this** once the card is in.

steps.title: Run the browser exploit
steps.body:
On the Wii U, open the **Internet Browser** and go to `wiiuexploit.xyz`.

Tap **Run Exploit!**, then hold the **B** button. Keep holding for about 20 seconds until the menu in the picture appears; you'll need it for the NAND backup next.

If the screen freezes or goes white, wait a few seconds; if nothing happens, reboot, clear the browser's saved data, and try again. If you land on the EnvironmentLoader instead, you didn't hold B long enough, so retry.

steps.title: Make a NAND backup
steps.body:
This is !!your safety net!!; if anything ever goes wrong, this backup can bring the console back.

Use the GamePad to highlight **nanddumper** and press **A**. On the next screen set it up like the picture: **SLC: Yes, SLCCMPT: Yes, OTP: Yes, SEEPROM: Yes**. Leave **MLC** on **No** unless you have a spare SD card bigger than your console's storage, since MLC is optional and not needed to fix a brick.

Press **A** to start the dump. When it finishes, power off the Wii U.

steps.title: Save your NAND backup
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of the Wii U and plug it back into this computer.

Press the button and Graphite grabs the backup the console just made (`slc.bin`, `otp.bin`, and the rest), copies it into a folder on your **Desktop**, and clears the files off the SD card to free up space. If a backup folder already exists, it saves a new one alongside it.

This is optional; you can skip ahead with **Next**, but keeping the backup is strongly recommended.

steps.title: Boot into Aroma
steps.body:
Run the browser exploit again exactly like before (`wiiuexploit.xyz` → **Run Exploit!**) but this time hold **X** instead of B. That opens the **Environment Loader**.

Press **A** on `aroma` to launch it. You'll see a warning that an update folder exists, and that's normal; press **X** to block updates. Then press **A** to load the **Wii U Menu**.

steps.title: Install the PayloadLoader
steps.body:
This makes Aroma reachable without the browser exploit every time.

Back on the Wii U Menu, find the new **PayloadLoader Installer** title (a rocket coming out of a box) and open it. Press **A** to check, and it should say the PayloadLoader can be installed onto the **Health and Safety Information** app. Press **A** to choose **Install / Update**, use the D-Pad to pick **Install**, and press **A**.

When it's done, press **A** to shut down the console.

steps.title: Make Aroma load automatically
steps.body:
From now on, turn the Wii U on and open the **Health and Safety Information** app to reach Aroma. To skip even that and boot straight into Aroma every time:

Open the Health and Safety app, launch `aroma`, then open the **PayloadLoader Installer** again. Choose **Check**, then **Boot options**, then **Switch to PayloadLoader**, and press **A** to shut down.

Boot the console once more. On the Environment Loader, press **Y** on `aroma` to set it as default, then **A**. On the Aroma Boot Selector, `Wii U Menu` is already highlighted, so press **Y** to make it the default, then **A**. Done: Aroma now loads on every boot, straight into the Wii U Menu.

(Hold **X** while booting to reach the Environment Loader again, or hold **START (+)** for the Aroma Boot Selector.)

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Aroma is installed and set to load automatically. You can drop homebrew apps into the `wiiu/apps` folder on your SD card and launch them right from the Wii U Menu.

Want online services back? Look into **Pretendo Network**. Want to manage saves? Grab **SaveMii** or the **Homebrew App Store** from the Homebrew App Store itself.
